risks: Dapr pub/sub component type 'pubsub.azure.eventgrid' is a Tier 2 component in Azure Container Apps — not listed as a GA Tier 1 pub/sub. Runtime behavior may be less reliable or unsupported compared to Tier 1 options like Service Bus.

No managed identity configured — the Dapr component uses key-based authentication (accessKey secretRef) and Cosmos DB access has no identity-based auth, increasing secret management burden and attack surface.
Public network access is enabled on both Event Grid topic and Cosmos DB account — data plane endpoints are exposed to the internet without network isolation.
Cosmos DB serverless is single-region only — 'enableAutomaticFailover: true' has no effect since serverless accounts cannot add secondary regions for failover.
No Application Insights resource — limits distributed tracing and end-to-end diagnostics for the event-driven pipeline.
No VNet integration on the Container Apps Environment — all inter-service traffic traverses public endpoints.
correctionsRecommended: Add a user-assigned managed identity to the Container App and configure the Dapr pub/sub component and Cosmos DB access to use managed identity instead of key-based authentication.
Consider switching the Dapr pub/sub component to 'pubsub.azure.servicebus.topics' (Tier 1 supported) or use Event Grid as an input binding ('bindings.azure.eventgrid') instead of a pub/sub component.
Add an Application Insights resource connected to the Log Analytics workspace for distributed tracing across the event pipeline.
Remove or set 'enableAutomaticFailover' to false on the Cosmos DB account since serverless capacity mode does not support multi-region failover.
Disable public network access on Cosmos DB and Event Grid; use private endpoints and managed identity when hardening for production.
Add an Azure Key Vault resource for centralized secrets management rather than inline secret references in the Dapr component.
